item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Kinda has an X-man feel to it but it's not a bad thing in moderation. If she could control the power I'd downvote, but I can see why this is so popular. No-vote from me, for being too much fantasy fulfillment and too little spook.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Live the music balloon and the pink whatever the heck.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It seems a little odd to me that descriptions of her appearance is used to imply a Mary Sue. Other than great beauty appearance is not a Sueish trait. And she's not described as very good looking (thank goodness, she's 8), she's described as very odd looking. Hell, the poor girl's hair keeps falling out, that's not a quality likely to win pageants.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I don't really think it matters now since this article is almost a decade old, but I'm gonna give my opinion of it anyway.
I'm not gonna lie, when I read, "Pink hair and different eyes" I got kinda worried she would be a Mary Sue. However, it's not really the case. With her abilities, the idea of someone turning one living think into another living thing is kinda cool, and she isn't OP. However, her apperence still leaves a Mary Sue vibe.
Still, it's kinda bare bones. I think if there were more experiments mentioned on what she was able to do and how it was observed it would seem more interesting. We only got descriptions of pre-existing creations, and reanimating a human body. Its not bad enough for a down vote, but it's, but not good enough for an upvote, so I'm staying nuetral.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Doesn't look like it. Just use 'view page revision' (V) and you're fine.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Darn, did I just do something? Sorry I'm trying to see if there's some way to look at old versions of a page.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I've… reread this, and it's still super-generic, seeming more like a comic book character than something appropriate for this site. We don't need to go back to the original by any means (As much as I like it, I think it had its chance and the verdict was that it didn't fit in anymore), but she needs some quirks — something odd about how her power functions, a distinctive physical trait, something.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-040 was one of fifteen subjects taken into custody: further investigation found no anomalous properties in any other individual

040 doesn't mention what number she herself had.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
SCP-040: Hmmm…They always served us peas for dinner, and I hate peas, so I always gave mine to Five, because she liked peas. But I think green beans are better.

What's Five?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Could you describe SCP-040-1-c better? I just think of a floating rubber ball with legs

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Eh. Bit too much on the X-men side for me, but it's not bad. Neutral.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I think that it was an underrated SCP article, but the rewrite killed just about everything there was to like about it in an attempt to darken it and make it less "Mary Sue."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It actually used to be less mundane, but as part of rewriting it to fit the modern tone and be less fluffy and eccentric, they took out most of what was actually unusual or interesting about her rather than exactly like every other little girl with magic powers, or exactly like every other "thing what makes other things" of a transformative nature. The rewrite got honestly bewilderingly positive response.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: This is an underrated scp article. it is a little unoriginal on the whole magical girl thing, but the way it's done, and what powers she has is what I like about it. this article needs more love!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's a little girl with magic powers.
Just like the five thousand other little girls with magic powers.

Its an extremely common and overused fictional trope, i find it hard to look past that. this just seems so…mundane

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I… honestly really dislike the rewrite? I guess it's a better fit for the modern tone, but she's just plain less interesting? The original was novel and fun, but this is just a bog-standard Thing What Makes Other Things of the transformative/mutation-y mold, except it's a little girl. I'm genuinely surprised and confused the response has been as positive as it has.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: She could become Thaumiel-Class :O

item.title: Cute!
item.content:encoded: Guys, THIS definitly is my new favorite SCP!!! <3

item.title: Staff Post - Closed
item.content:encoded: We don't roleplay on the discussion page. It's for opinions and critique on the article in question. We also don't allow sigs.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
please dont let me baby sit her again ….one tail is enough

Dr. tom

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Does anyone else think she sounds adorable?

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The ending cuts off a bit short. However, this was one of the first things I read here, and it's pretty cool to reread it. +1

item.title: Re: Well look at that, +100
item.content:encoded: Woops, forgot to upvote this one… 101 now.

item.title: Well look at that, +100
item.content:encoded: Best magical girl? Best magical girl.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I think at some point 040 will need to make an appearance in the bellerverse… give her a few thousand years to figure out the finer nuances of tinkering with biology, and at the very least, the ecosystem in her vicinity is bound to be…whimsical…

The words "Keter" and "Thaumiel" come to mind, depending on her disposition.

Also, theory:
The "Can only modify an organism once" limitation is because its a little like working with a 3d model, only far more detailed. Its fairly easy to start from scratch and control the general end result, but as soon as you start modifying an existing model it gets harder and harder to not end up with a distorted blob unless you know exactly what you're doing.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This needs mo example of what she can make

Just my 2¥

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: My first readthrough of this, I found her to be a complete and total Sue. Now, however, I see the truth of it. She's a little girl who grew up watching the cartoons with characters that look like she does now, like Sailor Moon and Tokyo Mew Mew. She hurt herself to change her outer appearence to match. That's actually quite sad.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: You must be seeing things there, 'cause he ain't Stitch.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The article is very well done indeed. +1. Especially SCP-040-1J. I noticed a homage to Lilo and Stitch when I read the description of SCP-040-1J.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Once again I find myself replying to old comments…

I liked this version of the article, but I didn't read the original. Regarding suggestions, the only thing I find myself particularly curious about when reading 040 is it's upbringing prior to containment.

There are some details there already, for sure. That being said, I would like to know what were "Mr. Green" and the rest of the crew up to. What was the purpose of the other fourteen subjects reported in the recovery log? Or is it right there and I'm the only one not seeing it?

Anyway, upvoted for good structure, precise description that doesn't drag on for too long and a hook that, despite needing some detailing, pulled me in.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
What? This is the result of "jumping on it", and quite a successful bit of jumping at that.

On top of that, it's kind of odd that you would say that the brilliance has been lost, despite not having read the original iteration to know if said iteration had brilliance in the first place.

I'm open to suggestions of improvement, but I'm going to need a bit more substantial critique than "someone needs to jump on this despite already having jumped on it".

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Great idea, but has been edited and reedited so many times it has lost its brilliance (though, in fairness, I never read the original. It might have always been boring). There is so much that can be done with concept, someone really needs to jump on this.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Thought it could use a little more, so I did a little cleanup and added an interview log.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: There probably needs to be some sort of link back to scp-040-1 somewhere. It looks orphaned right now.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's greatly improved from the tonal issues and silly addendums, but it seems rather bare-bones. I just can't find this interesting.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: No worries there. The physical flaws (blind eye, brittle hair) were from trying to do very small, very specific detail work, which, being eight, she is not very good at. Anything beyond that would be grimderp, rather than grimdark.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I came here to type almost the exact same thing, though with a caveat that it might not be necessary to add more examples. +1'ed as it is.

Try to keep 'em on the non-grimdark side, though. More to the point, she's a kid, don't be afraid to use 'cute-ish' examples. My $0.02

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Huh. I like it enough to remove my downvote, but I'm not sure I want to upvote it. Still greatly improved, and doesn't remind me of the decomms anymore.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I might do a supplement or extension or tale to that end, we'll see.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I enjoy this interpretation of it, but I wouldn't mind seeing more examples of what she's turned into things.

item.title: REWRITE UP
item.content:encoded: This article has been re-written, with clearance from Kain Pathos Crow.

item.title: Re: Rewrite
item.content:encoded: As said elsewhere: Permission granted, but run the draft past Kain first.

item.title: Re: Rewrite
item.content:encoded: One way to avoid grimdark would be by having her remain optimistic and cheerful. She's sure that she'll get the rest of her powers back someday. Weirder things have happened at the SCP Foundation, right?

item.title: Re: Rewrite
item.content:encoded: I volunteer my re-writing services.

item.title: Re: Rewrite
item.content:encoded:
Kain Pathos Crow 24 Oct 2012, 16:11

If you feel you can brush it up without making it grimdark, go ahead~

Voct, you have many talents, but I don't see how you would personally avoid the grimdark. Especially by making her chair. Let's leave this for someone else.

item.title: Rewrite
item.content:encoded:
I request permission to do a significant rewrite on this.

Specifically, I'd like to have the little girl have accidentally merged herself into her pet animated armchair SCP-040-1, and she's lost most of her powers as a result.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It feels like an older article that somehow slipped under the Mass Edit radar. -1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Sudden evolution is for the Pokemon world and is an oxymoron. I got halfway through but what I got is that she's cool-looking little girl with superpowers! -1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: If you feel you can brush it up without making it grimdark, go ahead~

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Another old article in need of brushing up.

-1 with hopes that someone will re-write.

item.title: Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
I'm calling for a review of this article. Please rate it and posts your thoughts on the discussion page here. Also consider SCP-040-1 and make related posts there.

The concept is interesting, but it's bogged down by poor writing and stupid addendums.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I have always thought of this as Baby Delerium from Neil Gaiman's Sandman. Essentially the same physical description, too.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
why aren't the "mental shutdown" effects of regular exposure to 148 manifesting in 040?

Alternative in-character answer: 040 is immune to the effect (possibly even suppresses it), and personnel hadn't figured out that effect independently.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
All personnel dealing with SCP-040 are to be perfectly content with their lives, and have no hidden or deep desires

Ruins it for me right there, in the first paragraph. And then, somehow, it gets worse.

item.title: Images
item.content:encoded:
I like this one but the lack of a cute photo hurt mah brain.
I'm pretty bad judging children's ages but I hope this photo can fit into 040's description.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
"Tests have deduced that subject has an abnormally high central internal body temperature and pressure, upwards of one thousand, six hundred and sixty eight degrees (1668) Celsius and six (6) kiloPascals"

Normal atmospheric pressure is 101 kiloPascals, so that's actually a reasonably low pressure, maybe it's meant to be 6 megaPascals? That would be 59.4 atmospheres, so more than high enough to count as abnormal.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I particularly liked all the added documentation. It gives 040 a more realistic nature when all progress (and lack thereof) is noted. Looks like it could go on for many more pages.
In regards to the above posts, I found the connection to 148 very intriguing. It could even use more details, but in no way I find it best removed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Yeah, I've been meaning to get around to asking the author to remove those.

You know what, consider this a request from the current author of SCP-148, directed at the author (or any staff that might have authority to make substantial edits) to remove any and all references to SCP-148 from SCP-040.

Alternatively, if this would be considered a minor edit, I can do so myself with staff permission.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Because this one uses an older version of 148, from before it had that effect.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Ok, so I checked this(it's nice…) and then went to check 148.

Then I came back again just to be sure I didn't miss anything and I still have one question: why aren't the "mental shutdown" effects of regular exposure to 148 manifesting in 040?
